narrative:
Happy Mother’s Day from First Warn Weather! 🌷

First Warn Forecast: Sunday, May 10, 2026
First Warn Headlines
Happy Mother’s Day! Cooler and comfortable conditions continue today.
A warming trend develops through the upcoming work week.
Slight shower and thunderstorm chances return Tuesday night.
Above normal temperatures are expected by next weekend.

Cooler air continues to settle into the region behind Saturday night’s cold front, making for a refreshing and comfortable Mother’s Day across Missouri and Illinois. Temperatures this afternoon will climb into the upper 60s to near 70 degrees under a mix of sunshine and scattered mid-level clouds. Northwest winds and cooler air aloft will keep things running about five degrees below average for early May standards.

Despite the cooler temperatures, today will be a pleasant day overall for cookouts, brunches, family gatherings, and outdoor celebrations. It’s the kind of spring day where opening the windows and enjoying the fresh air just feels right.

Tonight will turn noticeably cool once again as high pressure settles directly overhead. Clear skies, light winds, and dry air will create excellent radiational cooling conditions. Overnight lows will drop into the low and middle 40s across much of the bi-state region, with some sheltered valleys in eastern Missouri potentially slipping into the upper 30s by early Monday morning. You may even want a light jacket if you’re heading out late tonight or early Monday.

The warming trend begins Monday with abundant sunshine returning temperatures back into the lower and middle 70s. By Tuesday, southerly winds increase and much warmer air pushes back into the Midwest. Afternoon highs are expected to rise into the upper 70s and lower 80s across much of the region.

A cold front will move through Tuesday night bringing a scattered chance for showers and thunderstorms. Right now, widespread severe weather or heavy rainfall is not expected. In fact, rainfall totals look fairly light with many areas potentially receiving little accumulation at all.

Behind the front, temperatures Wednesday and Thursday should remain seasonable with highs generally near normal for mid-May. There may be some temperature differences from east to west depending on exactly how far cooler air pushes into the region.

Looking ahead toward next weekend, confidence continues to increase that warmer weather will build back into the Midwest as upper-level ridging expands eastward from the Rockies. High temperatures well into the 80s appear increasingly likely Friday into Saturday. While a few isolated showers or thunderstorms cannot be ruled out late week, the overall trend favors warmer and mainly dry conditions. At this time, widespread 90-degree heat still appears unlikely.

Enjoy the cooler spring air while it lasts because summer is slowly beginning to knock on the door.

Have a safe and blessed Mother’s Day!

— Dr. Nick
First Warn Weather
